name: Rundll32 with qwerty Argument Usage
notes: This rule triggers when rundll32 is executed with qwerty argument, which could indicate the presence of a Ransomware (type Locky).
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event QID is one of the following (5001828) Process Create, (5000862) Success Audit: A new process has been created
when the event matches Process Name IMATCHES 'rundll32\.exe' AQL filter query
when the event matches "Command" IMATCHES '.*qwerty.*' AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Rundll32 with qwerty Argument Usage on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event QID is one of the following (5001828) Process Create, (5000862) Success Audit: A new process has been created
AND when the event matches Process Name IMATCHES 'rundll32\.exe' AQL filter query
AND when the event matches "Command" IMATCHES '.*qwerty.*' AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: Possible Locky Ransomware detected based on rundll32 with qwerty argument
Event description: Possible Locky Ransomware detected based on rundll32 with qwerty argument
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
Add the "sourceIP" of the event or flow payload to the Reference Set "Compromised Hosts"
